subject: Databases
title: HAVING Filters Groups
description: A micro-lesson that makes HAVING Filters Groups usable.

hook.text: Show countries with more than 1,000 users. WHERE can't do it because WHERE runs before GROUP BY.

buildup.text: HAVING filters groups after aggregation. HAVING COUNT(*) > 1000 keeps only large groups.

discovery.text: WHERE filters rows before grouping. HAVING filters groups after. They're complementary, not interchangeable.

twist.text: Using WHERE for row-level filters and HAVING for group-level filters in the same query is common and correct.

climax.text: Find products with average rating above 4: GROUP BY product HAVING AVG(rating) > 4.

punchline.text: WHERE filters rows. HAVING filters groups. Know the difference.

quiz.question: When should you use HAVING instead of WHERE?
options: When filtering on an aggregate result that only exists after GROUP BY
When you want the query to run faster
HAVING and WHERE are interchangeable in all cases
